# PyPI publishing workflow (2026-01-01 10:51Z)

_**User (2026-01-01 10:19Z)**_

add a github actions workflow in order for me to later ship to pypl, you can follow something similar to this:



name: Publish to PyPI



# This workflow publishes the package to PyPI when a GitHub Release is created.

# It uses PyPI's trusted publishing feature, which must be configured on PyPI

# for the GitHub organization and repository.



on:

  release:

    types: [published]

  workflow_dispatch:

    inputs:

      version:

        description: 'Version to publish (e.g., 1.0.0)'

        required: false

        type: string



jobs:

  publish:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:

      id-token: write  # Required for trusted publishing (OIDC)

      contents: read    # Required to checkout code

    

    steps:

      - name: Checkout code

        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      

      - name: Set up Python

        uses: actions/setup-python@v5

        with:

          python-version: '3.11'

      

      - name: Install build dependencies

        run: |

          python -m pip install --upgrade pip

          pip install build setuptools wheel torch>=2.0.0

      

      - name: Build package

        run: python -m build

      

      - name: Check package

        run: |

          pip install twine

          twine check dist/*

      

      - name: Publish to PyPI

        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1

        with:

          packages-dir: dist/

---

---



---


Created the GitHub Actions workflow file at `.github/workflows/publish.yml`. It:

1. Triggers on GitHub releases (published) and manual workflow dispatch
2. Uses PyPI trusted publishing (OIDC) — no API tokens needed
3. Builds the package using `python -m build`
4. Validates the package with `twine check`
5. Publishes to PyPI using the official PyPA action

**Next steps to set up PyPI publishing:**

1. Configure trusted publishing on PyPI:
   - Go to your PyPI project settings
   - Add your GitHub repository as a trusted publisher
   - This enables OIDC authentication

2. To publish:
   - Create a GitHub Release, or
   - Use the "Run workflow" button in the Actions tab

The workflow installs `torch>=2.0.0` during the build step, matching your project dependencies. It uses Python 3.11, which is compatible with your `requires-python = ">=3.10"` requirement.

The workflow is ready to use once you configure trusted publishing on PyPI.

## Changes made

1. Removed `torch>=2.0.0` from build dependencies — it's a runtime dependency and not needed during build, which speeds up the workflow.
2. Added a cleanup step — removes old build artifacts (`dist/`, `build/`, `*.egg-info/`) before building to avoid conflicts.

## Verified compatibility

- Python version: 3.11 (project supports >=3.10)
- Build system: Uses `setuptools` matching your `pyproject.toml`
- Package data: `sheetsage/assets/*.json` will be included automatically
- Dependencies: Only installs build tools (`build`, `setuptools`, `wheel`)
- PyPI publishing: Uses trusted publishing (OIDC) — no API tokens needed
- Triggers: Works on GitHub releases and manual dispatch

The workflow is ready for PyPI publishing. After configuring trusted publishing on PyPI, you can publish by creating a GitHub release or running the workflow manually.

@@ -1,1 +1,102 @@
- 
+ # Repository Reset Guide
+ 
+ This guide will help you delete the current GitHub repository and create a new one with all your latest code.
+ 
+ ## Current Status
+ ✅ All local changes have been committed
+ ✅ Latest commit: &#96;3e79849&#96; - "feat: add PyPI publishing workflow and update project configuration"
+ ✅ Remote: &#96;https://github.com/openmirlab/sheetsage-infer.git&#96;
+ 
+ ## Step-by-Step Instructions
+ 
+ ### Step 1: Delete the Current GitHub Repository
+ 
+ 1. Go to your GitHub repository: https://github.com/openmirlab/sheetsage-infer
+ 2. Click on **Settings** (in the repository navigation bar)
+ 3. Scroll down to the **Danger Zone** section at the bottom
+ 4. Click **Delete this repository**
+ 5. Type the repository name &#96;openmirlab/sheetsage-infer&#96; to confirm
+ 6. Click **I understand the consequences, delete this repository**
+ 
+ ⚠️ **Warning**: This will permanently delete the repository and all its issues, pull requests, and wiki content. Make sure you have everything you need locally.
+ 
+ ### Step 2: Create a New GitHub Repository
+ 
+ 1. Go to https://github.com/organizations/openmirlab/repositories/new
+    (Or go to https://github.com/new if creating under your personal account)
+ 2. Fill in the repository details:
+    - **Repository name**: &#96;sheetsage-infer&#96;
+    - **Description**: &#96;Minimal inference-only version of SheetSage for music transcription&#96;
+    - **Visibility**: Choose Public or Private
+    - **DO NOT** initialize with README, .gitignore, or license (we already have these)
+ 3. Click **Create repository**
+ 
+ ### Step 3: Update Remote and Push Code
+ 
+ After creating the new repository, run these commands:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ cd /home/mku666/2025/chord_analysis/sheetsage-infer-latest/sheetsage-infer
+ 
+ # Remove the old remote
+ git remote remove origin
+ 
+ # Add the new remote (replace with your new repository URL if different)
+ git remote add origin https://github.com/openmirlab/sheetsage-infer.git
+ 
+ # Verify the remote
+ git remote -v
+ 
+ # Push all branches and tags to the new repository
+ git push -u origin master --force
+ 
+ # If you have tags, push them too
+ git push origin --tags
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Step 4: Verify Everything is Pushed
+ 
+ 1. Visit your new repository on GitHub
+ 2. Verify that:
+    - All files are present
+    - The commit history is intact
+    - The &#96;.github/workflows/publish.yml&#96; file exists
+    - The &#96;pyproject.toml&#96; has your latest changes
+ 
+ ### Step 5: Set Up PyPI Trusted Publishing (Optional but Recommended)
+ 
+ If you want to use the PyPI publishing workflow:
+ 
+ 1. Go to https://pypi.org/manage/account/publishing/
+ 2. Click **Add a new pending publisher**
+ 3. Fill in:
+    - **PyPI project name**: &#96;sheetsage-infer&#96;
+    - **Owner**: &#96;openmirlab&#96; (or your GitHub username)
+    - **Repository name**: &#96;sheetsage-infer&#96;
+    - **Workflow filename**: &#96;publish.yml&#96;
+ 4. Click **Add pending publisher**
+ 5. Approve the publisher on GitHub when prompted
+ 
+ ## Alternative: Keep Same Repository Name
+ 
+ If you want to keep the same repository name and just reset it:
+ 
+ 1. Delete the repository (Step 1 above)
+ 2. Create a new repository with the **same name** (Step 2 above)
+ 3. Follow Step 3 to push your code
+ 
+ ## Troubleshooting
+ 
+ ### If you get authentication errors:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Use GitHub CLI if installed
+ gh auth login
+ 
+ # Or use a personal access token
+ git remote set-url origin https://YOUR_TOKEN@github.com/openmirlab/sheetsage-infer.git
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### If you want to preserve issues/PRs:
+ - Export issues before deleting (GitHub Settings → Export repository data)
+ - Or use GitHub's repository transfer feature instead of deleting
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

  ## 📌 Overview
  
- **SheetSage-Infer** is a streamlined, inference-only version of [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage), optimized for easier deployment and integration as a library package with vendored Jukebox modules.
- 
- &gt; **Note**: This project is based on [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) by Chris Donahue. All credit for the original model and research belongs to the original authors.
- 
- ---
- 
- ## 🎉 What's New
- 
- - **Latest**: Inference-only version with vendored Jukebox modules, comprehensive test suite (75+ tests), and improved dependency management
+ **SheetSage-Infer** is an inference-only version of [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) for music transcription, optimized for easy deployment with vendored Jukebox modules.
  
  ---
```

  ## ✨ Features
  
- - ✅ **Vendored Jukebox Modules** - No external Jukebox dependency needed; works out of the box
- - ✅ **CPU &amp; GPU Support** - Handcrafted mel-spectrograms (CPU) or Jukebox embeddings (GPU)
- - ✅ **Beat Detection** - Automatic beat tracking and time signature estimation
- - ✅ **Melody Transcription** - Note sequences with pitches and durations
- - ✅ **Harmony Transcription** - Chord progressions with symbols and timing
+ - ✅ **Vendored Jukebox Modules** - No external Jukebox dependency needed
+ - ✅ **CPU &amp; GPU Support** - Handcrafted features (CPU) or Jukebox embeddings (GPU)
  - ✅ **Multiple Export Formats** - LilyPond notation, MIDI files, PDF generation
- - ✅ **Audio from URLs** - Support for YouTube, Bandcamp, and other audio sources
- - ✅ **Comprehensive Testing** - Full pytest test suite with 75+ tests
- - ✅ **Modern Python** - Compatible with Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12
- - ✅ **Simple API** - High-level &#96;sheetsage()&#96; function for easy transcription
+ - ✅ **Audio from URLs** - Support for YouTube, Bandcamp, and other sources
+ - ✅ **Simple API** - High-level &#96;sheetsage()&#96; function

```diff
@@ -193,34 +193,7 @@
  ---
  
- ## ✅ Changes from Original SheetSage
+ ## 🔄 Differences from Original SheetSage
  
- **SheetSage-Infer** has been modified from the [original SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) to make it more suitable for library use and easier to maintain.
- 
- ### Key Improvements
- 
- | Feature | Original | This Version |
- |---------|----------|--------------|
- | **Jukebox Dependency** | External, complex install | Vendored, works out of box |
- | **Test Coverage** | Limited | 75+ comprehensive tests |
- | **Python Support** | 3.12+ only | 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 |
- | **Build System** | Hatch | Setuptools (standard) |
- | **Dependency Pins** | Loose | Explicit versions |
- | **Documentation** | Basic | CLAUDE.md + test docs |
- 
- ### What We Maintain
- 
- - ✅ All core transcription functionality
- - ✅ Same neural network models
- - ✅ Same output formats (LeadSheet, LilyPond, MIDI)
- - ✅ Same API interface for &#96;sheetsage()&#96; function
- - ✅ Same theory classes (Note, Chord, Melody, Harmony, etc.)
- 
- ### What We Changed
- 
- - **Vendored Jukebox Modules**: Eliminates complex external dependency
- - **Library-First Design**: Optimized for &#96;pip install&#96; and programmatic use
- - **Comprehensive Testing**: Full pytest test suite with 75+ tests
- - **Better Dependency Management**: Explicit version pins and compatibility
- - **AI Assistant Ready**: Includes CLAUDE.md for Claude Code integration
+ This version includes vendored Jukebox modules (no external dependency), supports Python 3.10-3.12, and is optimized for library use. All core functionality, models, and algorithms remain unchanged from the [original SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage).
  
  ---
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
@@ -201,33 +201,5 @@
  ## 🙏 Acknowledgments
  
- ### Original Research by Chris Donahue
- 
- **SheetSage-Infer** is built upon the excellent work of [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) by Chris Donahue. The original SheetSage represents a major advancement in music transcription, achieving state-of-the-art results through hierarchical transformer architectures.
- 
- ### Research Paper
- 
- **[SheetSage: A Hierarchical Transformer for Audio to Lead Sheet Transcription](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage)**
- 
- This work introduced hierarchical music transcription with melody and harmony extraction, enabling high-quality lead sheet generation from audio.
- 
- ### Original Author
- 
- - Chris Donahue
- 
- ### About This Implementation
- 
- &gt; **Note**: This package was created to continue the excellent work by providing easier deployment, vendored Jukebox modules, and comprehensive testing, while preserving 100% of the original model quality and algorithms.
- 
- **What we maintain:**
- - PyTorch 2.0+ compatibility
- - Modern dependency management
- - Inference-only packaging
- - Comprehensive testing
- 
- **What remains unchanged:**
- - All model architectures (100% original)
- - All transcription algorithms (100% original)
- - All model weights (100% original)
- - All output formats (100% original)
+ Based on [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) by Chris Donahue. All credit for the original model, algorithms, and research belongs to the original author.
  
  ---
```

</details>

</tool-use>

  ---
  
- ## 🔄 Differences from Original SheetSage
+ ## 🔄 Changes from Original SheetSage
  
- This version includes vendored Jukebox modules (no external dependency), supports Python 3.10-3.12, and is optimized for library use. All core functionality, models, and algorithms remain unchanged from the [original SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage).
+ **SheetSage-Infer** has been modified from the [original SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) to make it more suitable for library use and easier to maintain.
+ 
+ ### Key Improvements
+ 
+ | Feature | Original | This Version |
+ |---------|----------|--------------|
+ | **Jukebox Dependency** | External, complex install | Vendored, works out of box |
+ | **Test Coverage** | Limited | 75+ comprehensive tests |
+ | **Python Support** | 3.12+ only | 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 |
+ | **Build System** | Hatch | Setuptools (standard) |
+ | **Dependency Pins** | Loose | Explicit versions |
+ 
+ ### What We Maintain
+ 
+ - ✅ All core transcription functionality
+ - ✅ Same neural network models
+ - ✅ Same output formats (LeadSheet, LilyPond, MIDI)
+ - ✅ Same API interface for &#96;sheetsage()&#96; function
+ - ✅ Same theory classes (Note, Chord, Melody, Harmony, etc.)
+ 
+ ### What We Changed
+ 
+ - **Vendored Jukebox Modules**: Eliminates complex external dependency
+ - **Library-First Design**: Optimized for &#96;pip install&#96; and programmatic use
+ - **Comprehensive Testing**: Full pytest test suite with 75+ tests
+ - **Better Dependency Management**: Explicit version pins and compatibility
  
  ---
```

</details>

</tool-use>

  ## 🙏 Acknowledgments
  
- Based on [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) by Chris Donahue. All credit for the original model, algorithms, and research belongs to the original author.
+ ### Original Research by Chris Donahue
+ 
+ **SheetSage-Infer** is built upon the excellent work of [SheetSage](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage) by Chris Donahue. The original SheetSage represents a major advancement in music transcription, achieving state-of-the-art results through hierarchical transformer architectures.
+ 
+ ### Research Paper
+ 
+ **[SheetSage: A Hierarchical Transformer for Audio to Lead Sheet Transcription](https://github.com/chrisdonahue/sheetsage)**
+ 
+ This work introduced hierarchical music transcription with melody and harmony extraction, enabling high-quality lead sheet generation from audio.
+ 
+ ### Original Author
+ 
+ - **Chris Donahue** - Original SheetSage creator
+ 
+ ### About This Implementation
+ 
+ This package was created to continue the excellent work by providing easier deployment, vendored Jukebox modules, and comprehensive testing, while preserving 100% of the original model quality and algorithms.
+ 
+ **What we maintain:**
+ - PyTorch 2.0+ compatibility
+ - Modern dependency management
+ - Inference-only packaging
+ - Comprehensive testing
+ 
+ **What remains unchanged:**
+ - All model architectures (100% original)
+ - All transcription algorithms (100% original)
+ - All model weights (100% original)
+ - All output formats (100% original)
  
  ---
```

Creating a guide for testing the GitHub Actions workflow using standard methods:


---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="write">

<details><summary>Tool use: **write** • Edit file: /home/mku666/2025/chord_analysis/sheetsage-infer-latest/sheetsage-infer/docs/WORKFLOW_TESTING.md</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 341, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,341 @@
- 
+ # GitHub Actions Workflow Testing Guide
+ 
+ **Date:** 2026-01-01
+ 
+ This guide covers standard methods to test and verify the PyPI publishing workflow before it runs on actual releases.
+ 
+ ## 🎯 Standard Testing Methods
+ 
+ ### Method 1: Local Simulation (Recommended - Fastest)
+ 
+ Run the exact commands from the workflow locally to catch issues early:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # 1. Clean environment
+ rm -rf dist/ build/ *.egg-info/ sheetsage.egg-info/
+ 
+ # 2. Set up Python (use same version as workflow)
+ python3.11 -m venv test_workflow_env
+ source test_workflow_env/bin/activate
+ 
+ # 3. Install build dependencies (matches workflow)
+ python -m pip install --upgrade pip
+ pip install build setuptools wheel
+ 
+ # 4. Build package (matches workflow step)
+ python -m build
+ 
+ # 5. Check package (matches workflow step)
+ pip install twine
+ twine check dist/*
+ 
+ # 6. Verify artifacts
+ ls -lh dist/
+ # Should see: sheetsage_infer-X.X.X-py3-none-any.whl and .tar.gz
+ 
+ # 7. Test install from built package
+ pip install dist/*.whl --force-reinstall
+ python -c "import sheetsage; print('✓ Package installs correctly')"
+ 
+ # 8. Cleanup
+ deactivate
+ rm -rf test_workflow_env dist/ build/ *.egg-info/
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Pros:**
+ - ✅ Fastest feedback
+ - ✅ No external dependencies
+ - ✅ Can test before committing
+ 
+ **Cons:**
+ - ❌ Doesn't test GitHub Actions environment
+ - ❌ Doesn't test OIDC authentication
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ### Method 2: Using &#96;act&#96; (Local GitHub Actions Runner)
+ 
+ Test the workflow in a local Docker container that mimics GitHub Actions:
+ 
+ #### Installation
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # macOS
+ brew install act
+ 
+ # Linux (using install script)
+ curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nektos/act/master/install.sh | sudo bash
+ 
+ # Or download from: https://github.com/nektos/act/releases
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ #### Basic Usage
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Test the workflow (dry-run, won't actually publish)
+ act workflow_dispatch -W .github/workflows/publish.yml
+ 
+ # Test with specific event
+ act release -W .github/workflows/publish.yml
+ 
+ # List available workflows
+ act -l
+ 
+ # Run with verbose output
+ act workflow_dispatch -W .github/workflows/publish.yml -v
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ #### Limitations
+ 
+ - ⚠️ OIDC authentication won't work (can't publish to PyPI)
+ - ⚠️ Requires Docker
+ - ⚠️ May have slight differences from actual GitHub environment
+ 
+ **Pros:**
+ - ✅ Tests actual workflow YAML syntax
+ - ✅ Tests GitHub Actions environment
+ - ✅ Catches workflow-specific issues
+ 
+ **Cons:**
+ - ❌ Requires Docker
+ - ❌ OIDC won't work locally
+ - ❌ Some actions may behave differently
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ### Method 3: Manual Workflow Dispatch (GitHub UI)
+ 
+ Test the workflow manually through GitHub's interface:
+ 
+ 1. Go to your repository on GitHub
+ 2. Navigate to **Actions** tab
+ 3. Select **Publish to PyPI** workflow
+ 4. Click **Run workflow** button
+ 5. Choose branch and click **Run workflow**
+ 
+ **Note:** This will actually attempt to publish if OIDC is configured. Use TestPyPI first (see Method 4).
+ 
+ **Pros:**
+ - ✅ Tests in real GitHub Actions environment
+ - ✅ Tests actual workflow execution
+ - ✅ No local setup needed
+ 
+ **Cons:**
+ - ❌ Requires pushing to GitHub first
+ - ❌ May publish to PyPI if configured
+ - ❌ Slower feedback loop
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ### Method 4: TestPyPI First (Recommended for Publishing)
+ 
+ Before publishing to real PyPI, test with TestPyPI:
+ 
+ #### Step 1: Create TestPyPI Workflow
+ 
+ Create &#96;.github/workflows/publish-test.yml&#96;:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;yaml
+ name: Publish to TestPyPI
+ 
+ on:
+   workflow_dispatch:
+ 
+ jobs:
+   publish:
+     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+     permissions:
+       id-token: write
+       contents: read
+     
+     steps:
+       - name: Checkout code
+         uses: actions/checkout@v4
+       
+       - name: Set up Python
+         uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+         with:
+           python-version: '3.11'
+       
+       - name: Install build dependencies
+         run: |
+           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
+           pip install build setuptools wheel
+       
+       - name: Clean previous builds
+         run: |
+           rm -rf dist/ build/ *.egg-info/ sheetsage.egg-info/
+       
+       - name: Build package
+         run: python -m build
+       
+       - name: Check package
+         run: |
+           pip install twine
+           twine check dist/*
+       
+       - name: Publish to TestPyPI
+         uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
+         with:
+           packages-dir: dist/
+           repository-url: https://test.pypi.org/legacy/
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ #### Step 2: Configure TestPyPI Trusted Publishing
+ 
+ 1. Go to https://test.pypi.org/manage/account/publishing/
+ 2. Add your GitHub repository as a trusted publisher
+ 3. Use the same workflow filename
+ 
+ #### Step 3: Test Installation from TestPyPI
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Install from TestPyPI
+ pip install --index-url https://test.pypi.org/simple/ sheetsage-infer
+ 
+ # Verify it works
+ python -c "import sheetsage; print('✓ TestPyPI package works')"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Pros:**
+ - ✅ Tests actual publishing process
+ - ✅ Tests OIDC authentication
+ - ✅ Safe (doesn't affect real PyPI)
+ - ✅ Tests package installation
+ 
+ **Cons:**
+ - ❌ Requires TestPyPI account setup
+ - ❌ Requires separate workflow file
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ### Method 5: Build-Only Test Workflow
+ 
+ Create a workflow that only builds and validates (never publishes):
+ 
+ Create &#96;.github/workflows/test-build.yml&#96;:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;yaml
+ name: Test Build
+ 
+ on:
+   pull_request:
+     branches: [master, main]
+   push:
+     branches: [master, main]
+   workflow_dispatch:
+ 
+ jobs:
+   test-build:
+     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+     
+     steps:
+       - name: Checkout code
+         uses: actions/checkout@v4
+       
+       - name: Set up Python
+         uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+         with:
+           python-version: '3.11'
+       
+       - name: Install build dependencies
+         run: |
+           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
+           pip install build setuptools wheel
+       
+       - name: Clean previous builds
+         run: |
+           rm -rf dist/ build/ *.egg-info/ sheetsage.egg-info/
+       
+       - name: Build package
+         run: python -m build
+       
+       - name: Check package
+         run: |
+           pip install twine
+           twine check dist/*
+       
+       - name: Upload artifacts
+         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+         with:
+           name: dist-packages
+           path: dist/
+           retention-days: 1
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ This runs on every PR and push, ensuring the build always works.
+ 
+ **Pros:**
+ - ✅ Automatic validation on PRs
+ - ✅ Catches build issues early
+ - ✅ Never publishes (safe)
+ - ✅ Fast feedback
+ 
+ **Cons:**
+ - ❌ Doesn't test publishing step
+ - ❌ Doesn't test OIDC
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🔍 Recommended Testing Strategy
+ 
+ ### Before First Release
+ 
+ 1. **Local Simulation** (Method 1) - Test build commands locally
+ 2. **TestPyPI** (Method 4) - Test actual publishing to TestPyPI
+ 3. **Manual Dispatch** (Method 3) - Test workflow execution on GitHub
+ 
+ ### Ongoing Maintenance
+ 
+ 1. **Build-Only Workflow** (Method 5) - Automatic validation on PRs
+ 2. **Local Simulation** (Method 1) - Quick checks before pushing
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## ✅ Verification Checklist
+ 
+ Before relying on the workflow, verify:
+ 
+ - [ ] Package builds successfully (&#96;python -m build&#96;)
+ - [ ] Package metadata is valid (&#96;twine check dist/*&#96;)
+ - [ ] Package installs from wheel (&#96;pip install dist/*.whl&#96;)
+ - [ ] Package imports correctly after installation
+ - [ ] Workflow YAML syntax is valid (no errors in GitHub Actions tab)
+ - [ ] OIDC is configured on PyPI (for actual publishing)
+ - [ ] TestPyPI publishing works (if using TestPyPI workflow)
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🐛 Common Issues
+ 
+ ### Build Fails
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Check pyproject.toml syntax
+ python -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml', 'rb'))"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Twine Check Fails
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Check package metadata
+ twine check dist/* --verbose
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Import Fails After Install
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Verify package structure
+ pip show sheetsage-infer
+ python -c "import sys; print(sys.path)"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 📚 Additional Resources
+ 
+ - [GitHub Actions Documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/actions)
+ - [PyPI Trusted Publishing](https://docs.pypi.org/trusted-publishers/)
+ - [act Documentation](https://github.com/nektos/act)
+ - [TestPyPI](https://test.pypi.org/)
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

+         uses: actions/checkout@v4
+       
+       - name: Set up Python
+         uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+         with:
+           python-version: '3.11'
+       
+       - name: Install build dependencies
+         run: |
+           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
+           pip install build setuptools wheel
+       
+       - name: Clean previous builds
+         run: |
+           rm -rf dist/ build/ *.egg-info/ sheetsage.egg-info/
+       
+       - name: Build package
+         run: python -m build
+       
+       - name: Check package
+         run: |
+           pip install twine
+           twine check dist/*
+       
+       - name: Upload artifacts
+         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+         with:
+           name: dist-packages
+           path: dist/
+           retention-days: 1
+

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,106 @@

+ #!/bin/bash
+ # Test the PyPI publishing workflow locally
+ # This simulates the exact steps from .github/workflows/publish.yml
+ 
+ set -e  # Exit on error
+ 
+ echo "🧪 Testing PyPI Publishing Workflow Locally"
+ echo "=========================================="
+ echo ""
+ 
+ # Colors for output
+ GREEN='\033[0;32m'
+ RED='\033[0;31m'
+ YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
+ NC='\033[0m' # No Color
+ 
+ # Check Python version
+ PYTHON_VERSION="3.11"
+ if ! command -v python${PYTHON_VERSION} &amp;&gt; /dev/null; then
+     echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  python${PYTHON_VERSION} not found, using default python${NC}"
+     PYTHON_CMD="python3"
+ else
+     PYTHON_CMD="python${PYTHON_VERSION}"
+ fi
+ 
+ echo "📦 Step 1: Cleaning previous builds..."
+ rm -rf dist/ build/ *.egg-info/ sheetsage.egg-info/
+ echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Cleaned${NC}"
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "🐍 Step 2: Setting up Python environment..."
+ VENV_DIR="test_workflow_env"
+ rm -rf ${VENV_DIR}
+ ${PYTHON_CMD} -m venv ${VENV_DIR}
+ source ${VENV_DIR}/bin/activate
+ echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Virtual environment created${NC}"
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "📥 Step 3: Installing build dependencies..."
+ python -m pip install --upgrade pip --quiet
+ pip install build setuptools wheel --quiet
+ echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Dependencies installed${NC}"
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "🔨 Step 4: Building package..."
+ python -m build
+ if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
+     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Package built successfully${NC}"
+ else
+     echo -e "${RED}✗ Build failed${NC}"
+     deactivate
+     rm -rf ${VENV_DIR}
+     exit 1
+ fi
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "📋 Step 5: Checking package metadata..."
+ pip install twine --quiet
+ twine check dist/*
+ if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
+     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Package metadata is valid${NC}"
+ else
+     echo -e "${RED}✗ Package check failed${NC}"
+     deactivate
+     rm -rf ${VENV_DIR}
+     exit 1
+ fi
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "📊 Step 6: Verifying artifacts..."
+ ls -lh dist/
+ ARTIFACTS=$(ls dist/*.whl dist/*.tar.gz 2&gt;/dev/null | wc -l)
+ if [ $ARTIFACTS -ge 2 ]; then
+     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Found $ARTIFACTS artifacts${NC}"
+ else
+     echo -e "${RED}✗ Missing artifacts (expected at least 2: .whl and .tar.gz)${NC}"
+     deactivate
+     rm -rf ${VENV_DIR}
+     exit 1
+ fi
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "🧪 Step 7: Testing package installation..."
+ pip install dist/*.whl --force-reinstall --quiet
+ if python -c "import sheetsage; from sheetsage.infer import sheetsage; print('✓ Import successful')" 2&gt;/dev/null; then
+     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Package installs and imports correctly${NC}"
+ else
+     echo -e "${RED}✗ Package installation or import failed${NC}"
+     deactivate
+     rm -rf ${VENV_DIR}
+     exit 1
+ fi
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo "🧹 Step 8: Cleaning up..."
+ deactivate
+ rm -rf ${VENV_DIR}
+ echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Cleaned up${NC}"
+ echo ""
+ 
+ echo -e "${GREEN}✅ All workflow steps passed!${NC}"
+ echo ""
+ echo "The workflow should work correctly on GitHub Actions."
+ echo "Note: This doesn't test OIDC authentication (PyPI publishing)."
+ echo "For that, use TestPyPI or the actual workflow with trusted publishing configured."
+ 
```

Created a testing setup for your GitHub Actions workflow. Summary:

## Created files

1. **`docs/WORKFLOW_TESTING.md`** — Testing guide covering:
   - 5 standard testing methods
   - Step-by-step instructions
   - Pros/cons of each method
   - Recommended testing strategy
   - Verification checklist

2. **`.github/workflows/test-build.yml`** — Test workflow that:
   - Runs on every PR and push
   - Builds and validates the package
   - Never publishes (safe)
   - Catches build issues early

3. **`test_workflow_local.sh`** — Local testing script that:
   - Simulates the exact workflow steps
   - Runs all build/check commands
   - Tests package installation
   - Provides colored output

## Recommended testing approach

### Quick local test (fastest)
```bash
./test_workflow_local.sh
```

### Before first release
1. Local simulation — Run `./test_workflow_local.sh`
2. TestPyPI — Create a TestPyPI workflow and test publishing there first
3. Manual dispatch — Use GitHub's "Run workflow" button

### Ongoing (automatic)
- The `test-build.yml` workflow runs automatically on PRs
- Validates builds without publishing

## Standard practices

1. Local simulation — Fastest feedback, catches most issues
2. TestPyPI first — Tests actual publishing safely
3. Build-only workflow — Automatic validation on PRs
4. Manual dispatch — Final verification before real releases

The guide includes troubleshooting tips and common issues. The `test-build.yml` workflow will automatically validate builds on every PR, ensuring the package always builds correctly before merging.
